Submodules

news.admin module

Django admin registration for news app models.

This module registers Publisher, Article and Newsletter models to the admin site and configures list display, search fields, filters, and custom admin actions for content moderation.

class news.admin.ArticleAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

actions = ['approve_articles', 'unapprove_articles']
approve_articles(request, queryset)[source]

Mark the selected articles as approved.

list_display = ('title', 'author', 'publisher', 'approved', 'created_at')
list_filter = ('approved', 'publisher', 'created_at')
property media
readonly_fields = ('created_at', 'updated_at')
search_fields = ('title', 'content', 'author__username')
unapprove_articles(request, queryset)[source]

Mark the selected articles as not approved.

class news.admin.NewsletterAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

filter_horizontal = ('articles',)
list_display = ('title', 'author', 'created_at')
property media
readonly_fields = ('created_at', 'updated_at')
search_fields = ('title', 'description', 'author__username')
class news.admin.PublisherAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: ModelAdmin

filter_horizontal = ('editors', 'journalists')
list_display = ('name', 'website', 'created_at')
property media
search_fields = ('name', 'description')

news.api_urls module

news.api_views module

REST API views and custom permissions for the news app.

This module defines viewsets for articles, newsletters, and publishers, plus a webhook endpoint for approved article notifications.

class news.api_views.ApprovedArticleWebhookView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: APIView

Internal webhook endpoint for approved article notifications.

permission_classes = [<class 'rest_framework.permissions.AllowAny'>]
post(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Handle POST notifications from the approval signal integration.

class news.api_views.ArticleViewSet(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelViewSet

CRUD API for articles with filtering, searching, ordering, and approval actions.

approve(request, pk=None)[source]

Toggle approval status for the specified article.

basename = None
description = None
detail = None
filter_backends = [<class 'django_filters.rest_framework.backends.DjangoFilterBackend'>, <class 'rest_framework.filters.SearchFilter'>, <class 'rest_framework.filters.OrderingFilter'>]
filterset_fields = ['approved', 'publisher']
get_queryset()[source]

Return articles visible to the current user, optionally filtered by author.

get_serializer_class()[source]

Use the list serializer for list views, otherwise use the detail serializer.

name = None
ordering = ['-created_at']
ordering_fields = ['created_at', 'title', 'approved']
perform_create(serializer)[source]

Attach the currently authenticated user as the article author.

permission_classes = [<class 'news.api_views.CanCreateContent'>]
search_fields = ['title', 'content', 'author__username', 'author__first_name', 'author__last_name']
subscribed(request)[source]

Return the authenticated reader’s subscribed article feed.

suffix = None
class news.api_views.CanCreateContent[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Permission rules for content creation, updates, deletion, and reading.

has_object_permission(request, view, obj)[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

has_permission(request, view)[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class news.api_views.IsEditorOrReadOnly[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Allow read-only access for authenticated users; editors may modify.

has_object_permission(request, view, obj)[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

has_permission(request, view)[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

class news.api_views.IsEditorPermission[source]

Bases: BasePermission

Allow access only to authenticated users with editor privileges.

has_permission(request, view)[source]

Return True if permission is granted, False otherwise.

basename = None

news.apps module

class news.apps.NewsConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]

Bases: AppConfig

default_auto_field = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField'
name = 'news'
ready()[source]

Override this method in subclasses to run code when Django starts.

news.filters module

Filter helpers for news article querysets.

This module provides shared filtering logic used by article list views, search pages, and admin filtering. It returns both the filtered queryset and a dictionary of active filters for display in templates and UI summaries.

news.filters.apply_article_filters(queryset, params, user=None)[source]

Apply user-facing filters to an Article queryset.

Parameters:

  • queryset – Initial Article queryset to filter.

  • params – Query parameters dict, typically request.GET.

  • user – Optional user instance for permission-aware filtering.

Returns:

(filtered_queryset, active_filters_dict)

Return type:

tuple

news.forms module

Forms used by the news app.

This module contains ModelForm definitions for creating and approving articles and for composing newsletters. Forms adapt their fields based on the requesting user’s role (reader/journalist/editor).

class news.forms.ArticleApproveForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]

Bases: ModelForm

Small form used to toggle approval status on an Article.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ('approved',)
model

alias of Article

widgets = {'approved': <django.forms.widgets.CheckboxInput object>}
base_fields = {'approved': <django.forms.fields.BooleanField object>}
declared_fields = {}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class news.forms.ArticleForm(*args, user=None, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelForm

Form for creating and editing Article instances.

  • Automatically hides the approved field for non-editors.

  • Restricts the publisher queryset based on the user’s role so that

    journalists and editors only see publishers relevant to them.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ('title', 'content', 'publisher', 'approved')
model

alias of Article

widgets = {'approved': <django.forms.widgets.CheckboxInput object>, 'content': <django.forms.widgets.Textarea object>, 'publisher': <django.forms.widgets.Select object>, 'title': <django.forms.widgets.TextInput object>}
base_fields = {'approved': <django.forms.fields.BooleanField object>, 'content': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'publisher': <django.forms.models.ModelChoiceField object>, 'title': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}
declared_fields = {}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

save(commit=True)[source]

Set the article author to the requesting user when present.

Saves the instance and returns it. If commit is False, the unsaved instance is returned (standard ModelForm behavior).

class news.forms.NewsletterForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelForm

Form for creating/editing Newsletter instances.

The articles field lists approved articles that can be added to the newsletter via checkboxes.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ('title', 'description', 'articles')
model

alias of Newsletter

widgets = {'description': <django.forms.widgets.Textarea object>, 'title': <django.forms.widgets.TextInput object>}
base_fields = {'articles': <django.forms.models.ModelMultipleChoiceField object>, 'description': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'title': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}
declared_fields = {'articles': <django.forms.models.ModelMultipleChoiceField object>}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

save(commit=True)[source]

Save this form’s self.instance object if commit=True. Otherwise, add a save_m2m() method to the form which can be called after the instance is saved manually at a later time. Return the model instance.

class news.forms.PublisherForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]

news.models module

class news.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

A single journalistic article authored by a journalist and optionally published.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

approved

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

author

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id
content

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)
get_next_by_updated_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: updated_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)
get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)
get_previous_by_updated_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: updated_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)
get_status_class()[source]

Return a CSS class name for the article approval status badge.

get_status_label()[source]

Return a human-readable status label for the article approval state.

id

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

newsletters

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>
publisher

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

publisher_id
save(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Save the current instance. Override this in a subclass if you want to control the saving process.

The ‘force_insert’ and ‘force_update’ parameters can be used to insist that the “save” must be an SQL insert or update (or equivalent for non-SQL backends), respectively. Normally, they should not be set.

title

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

updated_at

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

property was_just_approved

True when approved has changed from False → True (pre-save state).

property word_count

Return the total number of words in the article content.

articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

class news.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

A publishing organization that can own articles and

assign journalists/editors.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

property approved_articles_count

Return the number of approved articles associated with this publisher.

articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

created_at

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

description

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

editors

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>
subscribed_readers

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

website

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

news.serializers module

Serializers for the news application.

This module exposes concise and detailed serializers for Article, Newsletter, Publisher, and user profile summaries. Serializers include read-only summary representations for nested related objects and validation logic that enforces role-based constraints (e.g. only editors may approve articles).

class news.serializers.ArticleDetailSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Detailed serializer for Article create/retrieve/update actions.

Provides both the editable publisher primary key field and a read-only publisher_detail nested representation. Includes custom validation to enforce that only editors can set approved.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ('id', 'title', 'content', 'author', 'publisher', 'publisher_detail', 'approved', 'status', 'source_label', 'newsletter_count', 'created_at', 'updated_at')
model

alias of Article

read_only_fields = ('id', 'author', 'status', 'source_label', 'newsletter_count', 'created_at', 'updated_at')
get_newsletter_count(obj)[source]

Return the number of newsletters that include this article.

get_source_label(obj)[source]

Return the name of the publisher or ‘Independent’ when none exists.

get_status(obj)[source]

Return the article’s current status label.

validate(data)[source]

Enforce role-based validation rules on article data.

  • Only users with the editor role may set approved=True.

  • Non-editors who update an existing article will have approved forced to False to ensure changes enter the review queue.

class news.serializers.ArticleListSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Summary serializer for article lists and feeds.

Includes a short excerpt and a human-readable status label.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ('id', 'title', 'excerpt', 'author', 'publisher', 'approved', 'status', 'created_at')
model

alias of Article

read_only_fields = ('id', 'title', 'excerpt', 'author', 'publisher', 'approved', 'status', 'created_at')
get_excerpt(obj)[source]

Return a short excerpt derived from article content.

Grabs the first 25 words (or fewer) and appends an ellipsis when the content is longer.

get_status(obj)[source]

Return the article’s human-readable status label.

class news.serializers.NewsletterDetailSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Detailed serializer for creating and updating newsletters.

Accepts article_ids (write-only) to set the newsletter’s articles, and exposes a read-only articles list for retrieval.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ('id', 'title', 'description', 'author', 'articles', 'article_ids', 'article_count', 'created_at', 'updated_at')
model

alias of Newsletter

read_only_fields = ('id', 'author', 'articles', 'article_count', 'created_at', 'updated_at')
create(validated_data)[source]

Create a newsletter and attach any provided articles.

The article_ids are provided under the articles key in validated_data because of the source=’articles’ mapping.

get_article_count(obj)[source]

Return the number of articles included in this newsletter.

update(instance, validated_data)[source]

Update newsletter fields and optionally replace article M2M list.

class news.serializers.NewsletterListSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for displaying newsletter lists with article counts.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ('id', 'title', 'description', 'author', 'article_count', 'created_at')
model

alias of Newsletter

read_only_fields = ('id', 'title', 'description', 'author', 'article_count', 'created_at')
class news.serializers.PublisherDetailSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Detailed publisher serializer with editorial relationships.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ('id', 'name', 'description', 'website', 'editors', 'journalists', 'article_count', 'created_at')
model

alias of Publisher

read_only_fields = ('id', 'name', 'description', 'website', 'editors', 'journalists', 'article_count', 'created_at')
get_article_count(obj)[source]

Return how many articles are associated with this publisher.

class news.serializers.PublisherSummarySerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Compact publisher representation used in nested contexts.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ('id', 'name', 'website')
model

alias of Publisher

read_only_fields = ('id', 'name', 'website')
class news.serializers.UserProfileSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Exposes role-specific profile fields for different user roles.

Readers expose subscribed_publishers and subscribed_journalists. Journalists and editors additionally expose article/newsletter counts and independent counts. Fields that are not applicable for a role are returned as None to make API responses explicit.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ('id', 'username', 'full_name', 'role', 'bio', 'avatar_url', 'is_active', 'date_joined', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists', 'article_count', 'newsletter_count', 'independent_article_count', 'independent_newsletter_count')
model

alias of CustomUser

read_only_fields = ('id', 'username', 'full_name', 'role', 'bio', 'avatar_url', 'is_active', 'date_joined', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists', 'article_count', 'newsletter_count', 'independent_article_count', 'independent_newsletter_count')
get_article_count(obj)[source]
get_full_name(obj)[source]
get_independent_article_count(obj)[source]
get_independent_newsletter_count(obj)[source]
get_newsletter_count(obj)[source]
get_subscribed_journalists(obj)[source]
get_subscribed_publishers(obj)[source]
class news.serializers.UserSummarySerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Lightweight serializer for user references.

Used when embedding a user inside other serializers (e.g. article author). Provides a full_name field that falls back to username.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

fields = ('id', 'username', 'full_name', 'role')
model

alias of CustomUser

read_only_fields = ('id', 'username', 'full_name', 'role')
get_full_name(obj)[source]

Return a display name for the user, preferring full name.

news.signals module

Signal handlers and helpers for post-approval workflows.

This module performs post-approval tasks for Article instances, including: - Gathering subscriber email addresses (journalist- and publisher-subscribers), - Sending notification emails to subscribers, and - Posting a lightweight notification to an internal endpoint for further

integration (runs in a background thread to avoid blocking requests).

Signal handlers are intentionally tolerant of missing related data and network failures — logging warnings instead of raising errors so that the main application flow is unaffected by non-critical post-save actions.

news.signals.on_article_save(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)[source]

Signal handler triggered after an Article is saved.

The handler performs post-approval actions only when the article has just been approved. To avoid triggering on unrelated saves, the view or model code sets a _approval_just_toggled sentinel on the instance around the save() call; this handler checks that flag to decide.

news.tests_api module

API integration tests for the Newsroom application.

This test module covers role-based access control, reader subscription feed behavior, journalist workflow, editor approval actions, newsletter creation, and internal webhook/signal integration.

class news.tests_api.NewsroomAPITestSuite(methodName='runTest')[source]

Bases: APITestCase

API test suite for newsroom role, content, and workflow behavior.

authenticate_as(token)[source]

Helper to inject the Token into authorization headers.

setUp()[source]

Hook method for setting up the test fixture before exercising it.

test_editor_can_approve_article()[source]

Successful Action: Editor triggers custom nested approval action route.

test_editor_can_delete_article()[source]

Successful Action: Editor completely deletes an article resource.

test_journalist_can_create_article()[source]

Successful Request: Journalist creates a draft article.

test_journalist_cannot_approve_article()[source]

Failed Action: Journalist tries to approve their own article via API.

test_newsletter_crud_behavior()[source]

Tests complete lifecycle validation inside Newsletters.

test_non_reader_cannot_access_subscribed_endpoint()[source]

Failed Request: Journalist tries to load subscriber feed.

test_reader_cannot_create_article()[source]

Failed Request: Role guard blocks reader from using POST.

test_reader_cannot_see_pending_articles()[source]
Success/Failure boundary:

Reader sees approved articles but never drafts.

test_reader_retrieves_only_subscribed_content()[source]

Verifies GET /api/articles/subscribed/ restricts payload parameters correctly.

test_unauthenticated_request_fails()[source]

Ensure requests without a token are completely blocked.

test_webhook_signal_execution_on_approval(mock_post)[source]

Verifies that transitioning an article’s approval status correctly triggers automated backend integration pipelines using requests.

news.urls module

news.views module

news.views.api_docs_view(request)[source]

Render the API documentation page for authenticated users.

news.views.article_approval_queue_view(request)[source]

Displays all pending (unapproved) articles for editorial review. Restricted to users with the Editor role or Editor group membership.

news.views.article_approve_confirm_view(request, pk)[source]

Confirmation page before approving an article. Shows article metadata and a content preview.

news.views.article_approve_view(request, pk)[source]

Toggle approval on a single article. Editors only.

news.views.article_create_view(request)[source]

Handle article creation and redirect to the new article detail view.

news.views.article_delete_view(request, pk)[source]

Confirm and delete an article if the current user is authorized.

news.views.article_detail_view(request, pk)[source]

Render a single article detail page, enforcing reader access rules.

news.views.article_edit_view(request, pk)[source]

Handle editing an existing article when the user has permission.

news.views.article_list_view(request)[source]

Display a paginated, filterable list of articles for authorized users.

news.views.dashboard_view(request)[source]

Render the news dashboard with recent content and user-specific metrics.

news.views.newsletter_create_view(request)[source]

Handle newsletter creation and redirect to the new newsletter detail view.

news.views.newsletter_delete_view(request, pk)[source]

Confirm and delete a newsletter when the current user may do so.

news.views.newsletter_detail_view(request, pk)[source]

Show details for a newsletter and whether the user may edit it.

news.views.newsletter_edit_view(request, pk)[source]

Handle editing an existing newsletter when the user is authorized.

news.views.newsletter_list_view(request)[source]

Render a searchable, paginated list of newsletters.

news.views.publisher_create_view(request)[source]

Handles creating a brand new publisher profile.

news.views.publisher_delete_view(request, pk)[source]

Handles the safe deletion of a publisher network node.

news.views.publisher_detail_view(request, pk)[source]

Show a publisher with its articles, hiding drafts from readers.

news.views.publisher_list_view(request)[source]

Render a list of publishers including approved article counts.

news.views.publisher_update_view(request, pk)[source]

Handles editing an existing publisher profile.
